summary: Follow-up noted while reconciling the consolidated reviewer-validator report with current runtime semantics, readiness attention summaries, and the repo-local Python workspace contract.

observations.problem: Runtime and regression tests now separate empty/non-ready states with top-level attention semantics and readiness upstream summaries, but checked-in generated report snapshots can still retain pre-change pass-era values until a manual refresh step is run.
observations.why_it_matters: When stored snapshots lag behind runtime semantics, reviewers can reopen already-fixed issues, documentation has to explain snapshot versus code behavior manually, and public maintenance work becomes noisier than necessary.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a deterministic post-contract-change regeneration/parity step for canonical generated reports so status-semantic changes either refresh the stored artifacts or fail a narrow consistency check.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-10 KST: `make canonical-parity-guard` now runs script-output-surfaces, generated-artifact-index, archive-execution-manifest, artifact-freshness, fixed-point finalization, tmp cleanup, and a final post-check dry-run with `--fail-on-refresh-required` so checked-in canonical reports cannot keep pre-change semantics after the normal refresh chain. `make check-finalized` is now the documented full-vault closeout default.
